sonnet_review.biasedText: Last Thursday, she was prepared to do battle with Hill Democrats who, due to their dogmatic clinging to bad opinion, public policy proposals, and overall abject stupidity, failed to land a haymaker on the feisty Great Plains Republican.
sonnet_review.reason: This aligns with my detection of the same passage as Mudslinging/Ad Hominem. The model correctly identifies the emotionally charged language and combative framing.

sonnet_review.biasedText: Democrats who, due to their dogmatic clinging to bad opinion, public policy proposals, and overall abject stupidity
sonnet_review.reason: This is a valid sub-detection from the same passage I flagged. Presenting subjective judgments about Democrats' 'stupidity' and 'bad opinion' as fact is indeed opinion presented as fact.
Directly matches my detection of ad hominem attacks against Democratic lawmakers in this passage.
I flagged this same text as 'Opinion Statements Presented as Fact.' Categorizing it as spin is equally valid—it's a dismissive, opinionated characterization.
I didn't specifically flag this sentence on its own, but the casual, dismissive tone does constitute biased word choice. I had flagged the broader passage for bias by omission.

sonnet_review.biasedText: The liberal can whine and harass ICE all they want	—people who shouldn't be here are going back	—Adios, sayonara, annyeonghi gaseyo.
sonnet_review.reason: Directly matches my detection of this passage as spin with taunting, combative language and dismissive framing of opposition.

gpt_review.biasedText: due to their dogmatic clinging to bad opinion, public policy proposals, and overall abject stupidity
gpt_review.reason: Matches my detection of the same passage as containing ad hominem attacks and loaded language against Democrats.

gpt_review.biasedText: failed to land a haymaker on the feisty Great Plains Republican
gpt_review.reason: Matches my detection of subjective qualifying adjectives and combative framing that favors Noem over Democrats.

gpt_review.biasedText: if you're going to try to take a shot from the Grassy Knoll, be sure you have your stuff together.
gpt_review.reason: Directly matches my detection of this Grassy Knoll reference as loaded, inflammatory language characterizing congressional oversight as a sneaky attack.

gpt_review.biasedText: Oh, the media ate it up
gpt_review.reason: I did not specifically flag this phrase, but upon review it does dismissively characterize media coverage as uncritical, which is biased word choice.
I flagged the broader passage for bias by omission but not this specific sentence. The dismissive, informal tone is a defensible spin detection.
Directly matches my detection of this exact text and bias type.

gpt_review.biasedText: You cannot stop them. The courts can't. The Democrats can't. The liberal can whine and harass ICE all they want—people who shouldn't be here are going back—Adios, sayonara, annyeonghi gaseyo.
gpt_review.reason: Directly matches my detection of this passage as taunting, combative spin and partisan advocacy.

gpt_review.biasedText: Editor's Note: Do you enjoy Townhall's conservative reporting that takes on the radical left and woke media? Please support our work so that we can continue to bring you the truth.
gpt_review.reason: I did not flag this as it's an editor's note/fundraising appeal rather than article content. However, it does use loaded language ('radical left,' 'woke media') and frames their reporting as uniquely truthful. It's defensible to flag it, though it's technically a promotional sidebar.

sonnet_false_negatives.biasedText: Nice Try, Dems, But Your Little Stunt Against Kristi Noem Last Week Imploded in Seconds
sonnet_false_negatives.reason: Sonnet missed the headline's sensational, dismissive language ('little stunt,' 'imploded in seconds') that frames congressional questioning as a trivial failed trick.

sonnet_false_negatives.biasedText: Also, if you're going to try to take a shot from the Grassy Knoll, be sure you have your stuff together.
sonnet_false_negatives.reason: Sonnet missed this inflammatory JFK assassination comparison used to characterize Democratic congressional oversight.

sonnet_false_negatives.biasedText: Democrats tried to use the deportation of a military veteran of Korean descent to land a broadside hit at the Trump administration's mass deportation agenda.
sonnet_false_negatives.reason: Sonnet missed how framing Democrats' questioning as an attempt to 'land a broadside hit' characterizes legitimate oversight as a calculated attack.

sonnet_false_negatives.biasedText: Yeah, Park self-deported, guys. He had his green card revoked, had been under a removal order since 2009, and never once pursued citizenship.
sonnet_false_negatives.reason: Sonnet missed that the author presents only the counter-narrative without exploring the veteran's military service, Purple Heart, or any sympathetic context.
